segments.text: Then Deborah and Barak the son of Abinoam sang on that day, saying,
Because the leaders took the lead in Israel, because the people offered themselves willingly, be blessed, Yahweh!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Hear, you kings! Give ear, you princes! I, even I, will sing to Yahweh. I will sing praise to Yahweh, the God of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Yahweh, when you went out of Seir, when you marched out of the field of Edom, the earth trembled, the sky also dropped. Yes, the clouds dropped water.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The mountains quaked at Yahweh’s presence, even Sinai at the presence of Yahweh, the God of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“In the days of Shamgar the son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the highways were unoccupied. 
The travelers walked through byways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The rulers ceased in Israel. They ceased until I, Deborah, arose; 
Until I arose a mother in Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They chose new gods. Then war was in the gates. 
Was there a shield or spear seen among forty thousand in Israel?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
My heart is toward the governors of Israel, who offered themselves willingly among the people. 
Bless Yahweh!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“Speak, you who ride on white donkeys, you who sit on rich carpets, 
and you who walk by the way.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Far from the noise of archers, in the places of drawing water, there they will rehearse Yahweh’s righteous acts, 
the righteous acts of his rule in Israel. 
“Then Yahweh’s people went down to the gates.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
‘Awake, awake, Deborah! 
Awake, awake, utter a song! 
Arise, Barak, and lead away your captives, you son of Abinoam.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Then a remnant of the nobles and the people came down. Yahweh came down for me against the mighty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those whose root is in Amalek came out of Ephraim, after you, Benjamin, among your peoples. Governors come down out of Machir. Those who handle the marshal’s staff came out of Zebulun.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The princes of Issachar were with Deborah. As was Issachar, so was Barak. 
They rushed into the valley at his feet. By the watercourses of Reuben, there were great resolves of heart.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why did you sit among the sheepfolds? To hear the whistling for the flocks? At the watercourses of Reuben, there were great searchings of heart.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Gilead lived beyond the Jordan. Why did Dan remain in ships?
Asher sat still at the haven of the sea, 
and lived by his creeks.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Zebulun was a people that jeopardized their lives to the death; Naphtali also, on the high places of the field.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“The kings came and fought, then the kings of Canaan fought at Taanach by the waters of Megiddo. 
They took no plunder of silver.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: From the sky the stars fought. From their courses, they fought against Sisera.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The river Kishon swept them away, that ancient river, the river Kishon. 
My soul, march on with strength.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the horse hoofs stamped because of the prancing, the prancing of their strong ones.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
‘Curse Meroz,’ said Yahweh’s angel.‘Curse bitterly its inhabitants, 
because they didn’t come to help Yahweh, 
to help Yahweh against the mighty.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh's angel

segments.text:
“Jael shall be blessed above women, the wife of Heber the Kenite; 
blessed shall she be above women in the tent.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
He asked for water. She gave him milk. 
She brought him butter in a lordly dish.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
She put her hand to the tent peg, and her right hand to the workmen’s hammer. With the hammer she struck Sisera. She struck through his head. 
Yes, she pierced and struck through his temples.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
At her feet he bowed, he fell, he lay. At her feet he bowed, he fell. 
Where he bowed, there he fell down dead.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Through the window she looked out, and cried: Sisera’s mother looked through the lattice.‘Why is his chariot so long in coming? Why do the wheels of his chariots wait?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Her wise ladies answered her, Yes, she returned answer to herself,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
‘Have they not found, have they not divided the plunder? A lady, two ladies to every man; to Sisera a plunder of dyed garments, a plunder of dyed garments embroidered, 
of dyed garments embroidered on both sides, on the necks of the plunder?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “So let all your enemies perish, Yahweh, but let those who love him be as the sun when it rises in its strength.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the land had rest forty years.
